message.content:
You are verifier #2, independent. Read the ACTUAL code at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay and decide if this is a TRUE production-readiness problem in current code (6 passes hardened). confirmed=true only if real, reproducible, worth fixing (not speculative/by-design).
J-004: Check-then-create TOCTOU in _experiment_id loses the whole MLflow run under concurrent first-time start_run
File: src/assay_engine/observability/tracking.py:64
VERIFIED. _experiment_id() does get_experiment_by_name() then, if None, create_experiment() — a classic TOCTOU (lines 64-68). The first time a given experiment name is used, two concurrent start_run() calls can both see None and both call create_experiment(); MLflow rejects the loser with MlflowException(RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS), which propagates out of _experiment_id -> start_run. Reachable under the engine's own concurrency model: the reasoning seam runs an 8-thread pool, and multiple study processes legitimately share one local store. The consequence is exactly the lifecycle failure the rest of the module is engineered to avoid: in run_study a start_run exception leaves run_id=None, after which every track() call silently no-ops, dropping ALL metrics, the run_duration SLO metric, and provenance-trail artifact correlation for that entire study — and no MLflow run exists. The module's #F-023 orphan-termination logic targets phantom runs, but the experiment-creation step itself is unguarded, and no test covers the duplicate-create path.
PoC: Two threads call tracker.start_run('r', {}) for a brand-new experiment name (get_experiment_by_name == None for both); both enter create_experiment, one raises MlflowException(RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS). In run_study this surfaces as run_id=None -> all metrics for the study silently dropped.
Fix: Make resolution race-tolerant: wrap create_experiment in try/except that, on the duplicate-name error (RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS, or any failure), re-fetches via get_experiment_by_name(self._experiment) and returns its experiment_id; re-raise only if the re-fetch still returns None. Mirrors mlflow.set_experiment's get-or-create idiom. Add a test simulating create_experiment raising already-exists and asserting fallback.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	"""Experiment tracking — a local, on-box run/metric/artifact store (ADR-0003).
2	
3	Ported and generalized from the prior platform's MLflow setup. The backend is a local store
4	(default: a SQLite file under the working directory); a remote tracking server is rejected by
5	the local-URI guard. MLflow is optional (the ``observability`` extra) and imported lazily, so
6	this module loads without it.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	import os
12	from pathlib import Path
13	from typing import Any, Mapping, Protocol
14	
15	from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
16	
17	_DEFAULT_URI = "sqlite:///assay_mlflow.db"
18	EXPERIMENT_NAME = os.environ.get("ASSAY_EXPERIMENT", "assay")
19	
20	
21	def get_tracking_uri() -> str:
22	    """Resolve the tracking URI (env override, else local SQLite), enforced local."""
23	    uri = os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRACKING_URI", _DEFAULT_URI)
24	    # Resolve a relative sqlite path to an absolute file so runs land deterministically.
25	    prefix = "sqlite:///"
26	    if uri.startswith(prefix):
27	        rel = uri[len(prefix) :]
28	        if not os.path.isabs(rel):
29	            uri = prefix + str((Path.cwd() / rel).resolve())
30	    return require_local_uri(uri, what="experiment tracking")
31	
32	
33	# Structural Protocol only — adapter/seam validation is behavior-based, not isinstance (#148).
34	class ExperimentTracker(Protocol):
35	    def start_run(self, name: str, params: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str: ...
36	    def log_metric(self, run_id: str, key: str, value: float) -> None: ...
37	    def log_artifact(self, run_id: str, path: str) -> None: ...
38	    def end_run(self, run_id: str, status: str = "FINISHED") -> None: ...
39	
40	
41	class MlflowExperimentTracker:
42	    """A local-store experiment tracker backed by MLflow (lazy import).
43	
44	    Construction validates the tracking URI is local and selects the experiment; it does not
45	    import MLflow until a run is started, so the module is importable without the extra.
46	    """
47	
48	    def __init__(self, experiment: str = EXPERIMENT_NAME) -> None:
49	        self._experiment = experiment
50	        self._uri = get_tracking_uri()  # validates local before anything else
51	
52	    def _client(self) -> Any:
53	        """An MlflowClient bound to the local store. Uses the client API (keyed by run_id)
54	        throughout, so no run ever sits on the global active-run stack — start_run followed by
55	        log_metric cannot collide (audit issue #O1)."""
56	        try:
57	            from mlflow.tracking import MlflowClient
58	        except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
59	            raise RuntimeError(
60	                "experiment tracking requires the 'observability' extra (mlflow) — not installed"
61	            ) from exc
62	        return MlflowClient(tracking_uri=self._uri)
63	
64	    def _experiment_id(self, client: Any) -> str:
65	        exp = client.get_experiment_by_name(self._experiment)
66	        if exp is not None:
67	            return str(exp.experiment_id)
68	        return str(client.create_experiment(self._experiment))
69	
70	    def start_run(self, name: str, params: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str:
71	        client = self._client()
72	        run = client.create_run(self._experiment_id(client), run_name=name)
73	        run_id = str(run.info.run_id)
74	        # The run now exists in the store. If param-logging fails partway (network blip, server
75	        # error), the run would otherwise be orphaned permanently in RUNNING state — phantom runs
76	        # accumulate over time (pass 3, #F-023). Terminate it FAILED on any param-logging error
77	        # before re-raising, so a created run is never left dangling.
78	        try:
79	            for k, v in dict(params).items():
80	                client.log_param(run_id, k, v)
81	        except Exception:
82	            try:
83	                client.set_terminated(run_id, status="FAILED")
84	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001,S110 - best-effort cleanup; original error re-raised
85	                pass
86	            raise
87	        return run_id
88	
89	    def log_metric(self, run_id: str, key: str, value: float) -> None:
90	        self._client().log_metric(run_id, key, value)
91	
92	    def log_artifact(self, run_id: str, path: str) -> None:
93	        self._client().log_artifact(run_id, path)
94	
95	    def end_run(self, run_id: str, status: str = "FINISHED") -> None:
96	        # MLflow run statuses: FINISHED / FAILED / KILLED. Recording FAILED makes a failed run
97	        # distinguishable from a successful one in the store (#110).
98	        self._client().set_terminated(run_id, status=status)
99

content.text: The consumer behavior is exactly as described. Lines 351-373 confirm: a `start_run` exception leaves `run_id=None`, after which every `track()` no-ops (line 319), dropping all metrics. Now let me verify the concurrency claims — the 8-thread reasoning pool.

content.text:
Confirmed. The test exercises `get_experiment_by_name` returning None and `create_experiment` succeeding, but there is no test for `create_experiment` raising RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS (the duplicate-create path). The finding's claims all check out.

Let me confirm one nuance: whether MLflow actually raises on duplicate create_experiment (it does — this is standard MLflow behavior with a RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS error code), and assess the real-world likelihood/severity.

The finding is accurate on every verifiable point:
- Lines 64-68: genuine check-then-create with no race handling.
- The 8-worker `ThreadPoolExecutor` exists (seam.py:191-192), and multiple study processes sharing one local SQLite store is the documented model.
- Consumer impact at pipeline.py:351-373 is exactly as described: `start_run` exception → `run_id=None` → all `track()` calls no-op (line 319) → all metrics, run_duration SLO metric, and provenance artifact correlation silently dropped, with no MLflow run created.
- No test covers the duplicate-create path.

Severity assessment: This is a real but narrow window. The TOCTOU only fires on the *first-ever* use of an experiment name (once created, the `get_experiment_by_name` branch wins thereafter). So it is a one-time-per-experiment-name race, not a recurring one. The blast radius is one study's observability data (best-effort by design — the run itself does not abort), not core correctness or data integrity. The fix is trivial and matches MLflow's own `set_experiment` idiom. That places it at medium: real, reproducible, worth fixing, but bounded window and observability-only impact.

input.reasoning: Confirmed in current code. tracking.py:64-68 is a genuine check-then-create TOCTOU: _experiment_id() calls get_experiment_by_name() and, if None, create_experiment() with no race handling. Two concurrent first-time start_run() calls for a brand-new experiment name can both see None and both call create_experiment(); MLflow rejects the loser with RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS, which propagates out of _experiment_id -> start_run (note: it propagates before create_run, so unlike the #F-023 path no orphan run is even created here — the experiment-creation step is entirely unguarded). The concurrency preconditions hold: an 8-worker ThreadPoolExecutor exists (reasoning/seam.py:191-192) and multiple study processes sharing one local SQLite store is the documented model. Consumer impact is exactly as described: pipeline.py:351-373 catches the start_run exception, leaves run_id=None (commented inline at line 361), after which the track() helper no-ops on run_id is None (line 319), so all metrics, the run_duration_s SLO metric (line 727), and the provenance-trail artifact correlation (lines 731+) are silently dropped for that study. No test covers the duplicate-create path — test_observability.py:139-144 only exercises create_experiment succeeding. The proposed fix (try/except around create_experiment, re-fetch via get_experiment_by_name on duplicate-name error, mirroring mlflow.set_experiment's get-or-create idiom) is correct and minimal. Severity medium rather than high: the race window is one-time-per-experiment-name (once created, the get_experiment_by_name branch always wins), and the impact is observability data for a single study, which the module already treats as strictly best-effort (the run itself never aborts). Real, reproducible, worth fixing, but bounded window and non-correctness impact.

content.text: Confirmed J-004 as a real, medium-severity production issue. Details captured in the StructuredOutput call above. Key file: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/observability/tracking.py:64-68 (the unguarded check-then-create), with consumer impact at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py:351-373 and 317-322.
